guide_review_response.work_item: 2.4 closure (f1 fix corrections) + 2.5 (PS1 corrections) -- post-correction re-verification

reviewing.ea_work: Three required corrections (g5-7 dup tests, g5-8 env-fallback, g5-9 McpConfigPath) + encoding regression fix

guide_review_response.summary:
Independent disk verification confirms ALL three required corrections from the prior APPROVED_WITH_ADJUSTMENTS are correctly applied, plus EA-4 proactively caught and fixed an encoding regression. Invariants HOLD (HEAD still 9d17e7f, zero new commits; stash byte- identical; fleet_paused=true preserved; pytest tools/ confirms 282 passed). The work is clean and ready to proceed to Item 2.5.v2 with the expanded env-fallback functional test scope. No further corrections required.
Specifically: (1) g5-7: tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py is now 75 lines (was 95 with duplicates); the two new tests (test_require_project_id_rejects_zero, test_require_project_id_rejects_negative) appear ONCE each at lines 57-74. The duplicate copy at lines 76-94 is GONE. (2) g5-8: All 6 modified PS1 scripts now have the canonical env-fallback default expression `$(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' })` on the primary param. Verified per-file by direct read. (3) g5-9: $McpConfigPath REMOVED from param block in both agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 and la_merge_approve.ps1; computed at script-body entry as `$McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json'` with explanatory comment citing g5-9 + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW Section 3 G-1 fix #1. (4) Encoding regression: la_merge_approve.ps1 + agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 comments use "Section 3" (ASCII) not "§3" (non-ASCII). EA-4's proactive catch-and-fix is good defensive practice. (5) g5-10: A11 commitment self-tracked as EA-4 todo 15 for 2.10 STATUS append; will be verified at 2.10 closure.
EA-4 may proceed directly to Item 2.5.v2 verification.

independent_disk_verification.check: git log --oneline -1: 9d17e7f (zero new commits since EA-4 began this turn). git status: 30 entries (unchanged from prior verification). git stash list: stash@{0}: ...wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- byte-identical. pytest tools/ --tb=short -q: 282 passed in 2.68s. Single-commit discipline (w13) holds; fleet pause invariant holds; stash invariant holds.

Read tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py end-to-end: now exactly 75 lines (was 95). Lines 57-63: test_require_project_id_rejects_zero (single definition). Lines 66-74: test_require_project_id_rejects_negative (single definition). File ends at line 75 (no second copy). The duplicate block at the prior lines 76-94 is DELETED. pytest still 282 (no count change because duplicates were collapsing).

Direct disk-read of all 6 modified PS1 scripts confirms canonical env-fallback default:
wake_launcher.ps1 line 31: [string]$RepoRoot = $(if ($env:BLARAI_ROOT) { $env:BLARAI_ROOT } else { 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' }) agents-cadence-monitor.ps1 line 18: same expression escalation_watchdog.ps1 line 33: same expression toast_watchdog.ps1 line 15: same expression la_merge_approve.ps1 line 39: same expression test_async_post_gate.ps1 line 26: same expression (note: canonical primary $BlarAIRoot direction with [Alias('RepoRoot')] + shim $RepoRoot = $BlarAIRoot at line 30 -- intentional alias-direction inversion vs the 5 alias-on-existing-RepoRoot scripts; A11 documented deviation per g5-10.)
Per Stage 2 XML 2.5.v2 + iteration-2 g4-2 binding ack canonical signature_pattern_ps1. EA-4's claim "AST-verified env-fallback present on every script; runtime semantics verified across all 3 precedence cases" is supported by direct file reads.

agents-cadence-monitor.ps1: - Param block (lines 13-19): $McpConfigPath REMOVED. Param block now contains $ActiveMinutes, $IdleMinutes, $AgentGatesProject, [Alias('BlarAIRoot')] [string]$RepoRoot. - Lines 23-26: comment block "Stage 2.5 g5-9: derive $McpConfigPath from $RepoRoot ... (WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Section 3 G-1 fix #1.)" + computation `$McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json'`.
la_merge_approve.ps1: - Param block (lines 33-40): $McpConfigPath REMOVED. Param block now contains $Branch, $TrackingTaskId, $FleetReportsTaskId, $Summary, [Alias('BlarAIRoot')] [string]$RepoRoot. - Lines 44-47: comment block + computation `$McpConfigPath = Join-Path $RepoRoot '.mcp.json'`.
Functional impact: callers passing `-BlarAIRoot 'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform'` now get $McpConfigPath = 'C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\.mcp.json' (cross-project consistency). WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW Section 3 G-1 fix #1 explicit instruction honored.

Both modified comment blocks use "Section 3" (ASCII-clean) instead of the non-ASCII "§3" character. EA-4 caught and fixed this transient regression themselves before reporting, noting it in the report as "One transient regression (la_merge_approve.ps1 non-ASCII § in comment) caught + fixed (replaced with 'Section'); both McpConfigPath comments now ASCII-clean." Good defensive practice -- non-ASCII characters in PS1 source can interact badly with default Windows code pages depending on how the script is invoked. ASCII-only avoids that class of issue.

EA-4 reports A11 commitment "Queued as todo 15 for 2.10 STATUS append." Self-tracking commitment. Will be verified at 2.10 closure when STATUS.md is appended with the Stage 2 closure entry. No action needed now.

guide_review_response.continue_recommendation_unchanged: PROCEED to Item 2.5.v2 verification with the expanded env-fallback functional test scope per the prior Guide-#5 disposition: - Per-PS1 baseline: invoke with no -BlarAIRoot/-RepoRoot, confirm hardcoded default applies (BlarAI path). - Per-PS1 env-fallback: with $env:BLARAI_ROOT set, invoke with no -BlarAIRoot/-RepoRoot, confirm env value takes precedence over hardcoded default. - Per-PS1 alias binding: invoke with -BlarAIRoot 'X', confirm primary param ($RepoRoot on 5 scripts; $BlarAIRoot on test_async_post_gate) receives 'X'. - For agents-cadence-monitor + la_merge_approve: invoke with -BlarAIRoot 'C:\test', confirm $McpConfigPath resolves to 'C:\test\.mcp.json' (g5-9 functional verification). After 2.5.v2 closes, RE-EVALUATE budget honestly at the 2.5.v2 → 2.6 boundary. 2.6 is structurally comparable to file 13 (extended grep + g5-4 closure + D3 closure + observation o1 handler-guards-vs-VikunjaClient-routing). If budget is healthy, push into 2.6; if even slightly tight, emit v4 mid-stage handoff per i1-4 / dn15 (docs-only commit, no partial-commit refactor).
guide_review_response.proceed_authorization: APPROVED -- f1 fix + Item 2.5 corrections are fully verified on disk. EA-4 may proceed directly to Item 2.5.v2 verification. Standing reminders unchanged: single Stage-2 commit at 2.9 (now containing f1 fix + corrected PS1 work + future 2.5.v2 verification evidence + 2.6 + 2.7 + 2.7.v2 + 2.8 work); fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; append-only STATUS at 2.10 (now including A11 alias-vs-rename anomaly entry per g5-10); g5-4 legacy file disposition pending at 2.6; D3 dashboard_maintainer closure at 2.6; observation o1 handler-guards-vs-VikunjaClient-routing decision at 2.6. On 2.5.v2 closure, paste the verification evidence back to LA for Guide-#5 review + the 2.5.v2 → 2.6 boundary budget re-evaluation.
